pr.title: Rename ftCo_ItemGet to ftpickupitem, cleanup statics, match some functions
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Renamed the common fighter item-get module from ftCo_ItemGet to ftpickupitem based on an assert string that explicitly named ftpickupitem.c, propagated the symbol/file rename through C, assembly, configs, splits, symbols, object lists, and action-state tables, removed stale statics left from prior matching work, and matched several previously outstanding functions in the item pickup path.
what_changed: Renamed the module file from ftCo_ItemGet to ftpickupitem in both source and assembly paths: asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_ItemGet.s became asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftpickupitem.s, and the C/header side moved to ftpickupitem.c and ftpickupitem.h.
Replaced ftCo_ItemGet.h with ftpickupitem.h, preserving the header role while aligning it to the new file/module name.

Renamed many exported and local symbols from the ftCo_80094xxx / ftCo_ItemGet_* pattern to ftpickupitem_* equivalents, including ftpickupitem_80094150, ftpickupitem_800942A0, ftpickupitem_8009447C, ftpickupitem_80094694, ftpickupitem_80094790, ftpickupitem_80094818, ftpickupitem_800948A8, ftpickupitem_Anim, ftpickupitem_IASA, ftpickupitem_Phys, and ftpickupitem_Coll.
Updated action-state callback tables in ftdata so the item-get state now references ftpickupitem_Anim, ftpickupitem_IASA, ftpickupitem_Phys, and ftpickupitem_Coll instead of ftCo_ItemGet_* callbacks.
Propagated symbol renames into dependent assembly files, including ftCo_0A01.s, ftKb_Init.s, ftLk_SpecialN.s, ft_0CDD.s, ft_0D14.s, ftdata.s, and ftswing.s.
Propagated symbol/header usage into dependent C files across common fighter attacks and character-specific files such as Fox, GameWatch, Link, and Peach.
Updated build/config metadata in config/GALE01/symbols.txt, scratches.txt, splits.txt, configure.py, and obj_files.mk to track the renamed module and symbols.
According to the PR body, removed leftover static declarations or definitions that were artifacts of earlier matching work.

According to the PR body, matched some outstanding functions in the item-get file; the excerpt does not identify each matched function beyond showing reduced C/asm churn in ftpickupitem.c and ftpickupitem.s.
smart_moves: Used an in-binary/assert-string clue as naming evidence: the PR body says an assert string explicitly contained ftpickupitem.c, justifying the file/module rename instead of guessing from behavior alone.

Performed a repo-wide propagation of the rename, touching C call sites, assembly branch targets, symbol maps, splits, scratches, build configuration, and object lists in the same PR so the new module name stayed consistent.

Kept action-state callback names aligned with the renamed file prefix, using ftpickupitem_Anim, ftpickupitem_IASA, ftpickupitem_Phys, and ftpickupitem_Coll rather than mixing old ftCo_ItemGet callback names with the new filename.

Renamed address-suffixed functions conservatively by changing only the module prefix while retaining the address suffix, preserving searchability and decomp traceability for unmatched or semi-understood routines.
Cleaned stale statics while doing matching work, reducing old matching scaffolding instead of letting duplicate or unnecessary local declarations linger after functions became matchable.
decomp_lessons: Assert strings can provide strong evidence for original file/module names; when an assert explicitly names a C file, prefer that over provisional subsystem names.

A module rename in this codebase is not just a source-file rename: update assembly labels, C prototypes/includes, symbols.txt, scratches.txt, splits.txt, configure.py, obj_files.mk, and any data tables that store function pointers.
For still-address-named routines, preserve the numeric suffix during prefix cleanup; this makes the rename safer while maintaining cross-reference continuity.
Action-state callback names should follow the owning module prefix consistently, especially for Anim, IASA, Phys, and Coll functions referenced from ftdata.
After matching functions, revisit old static declarations and matching scaffolding. Leftover statics can obscure true linkage and make future symbol propagation harder.
When assembly remains in the repo for neighboring or dependent code, direct branch labels must be updated along with C declarations to prevent stale symbol references.
naming_conventions: The module prefix changed from ftCo_ItemGet / ftCo_ to ftpickupitem for this item pickup common fighter code.
Callback convention after the rename is ftpickupitem_Anim, ftpickupitem_IASA, ftpickupitem_Phys, and ftpickupitem_Coll.
Address-based helper names retained their address suffix under the new prefix, e.g. ftCo_80094790 became ftpickupitem_80094790.
The new filename is lowercase ftpickupitem.c / ftpickupitem.h, matching the assert-string evidence cited in the PR body.
The rename avoided inventing semantic names for individual helpers where only address-suffixed names were available in the excerpt.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Updated .fn and .endfn labels in the renamed assembly file to match the new ftpickupitem_* symbols.
Updated branch/call targets in assembly from ftCo_* and ftCo_ItemGet_* names to ftpickupitem_* names without changing instruction bytes or control flow in the excerpt.
Updated function-pointer entries in ftdata.s from ftCo_ItemGet_* callbacks to ftpickupitem_* callbacks.
Changed symbol references used in address materialization, such as ftCo_80094DF8@ha/@l and ftCo_80094D90@ha/@l, to ftpickupitem_80094DF8@ha/@l and ftpickupitem_80094D90@ha/@l.
Used cleanup of stale statics as part of matching outstanding functions, though the PR slice does not provide enough detail to enumerate the exact matching tricks for each function.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
No explicit reviewer feedback or requested changes were available in the slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further rename ftpickupitem_80094xxx helpers to semantic names.
Search for remaining ftCo_ItemGet references after PR 1426 to verify rename completeness.
Inspect ftpickupitem.c around the matched functions to identify which statics were removed and what exact matching patterns were used.
Look for additional assert strings that reveal original lowercase module filenames in ftCommon.
Compare ftdata action-state callback naming conventions for other common fighter states with ftpickupitem_Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll.
